title: Downstream Consumer Validation Implementation Tasks
principles: Prove consumer behaviour from outside the monorepo before publication.
Keep SDK downstream validation owned by packages/sdk/typescript while packages/scripts remains planned.
Keep apps/api as the live process owner and @whattax/api-http as the HTTP contract and client owner.
Treat unresolved workspace protocol dependencies in packed manifests as release-blocker evidence, not as a reason to weaken validation.
Reuse canonical schemas, descriptors, tagged errors and SDK/API exports instead of writing local DTO mirrors in the downstream workspace.
Require parent review, final call-graph verification and three documented improvement audit passes before accepting each implementation slice.
beforeImplementation: Create an active execution plan under docs/exec-plans/active/ before editing runtime, package scripts or package docs.
Set one comprehensive goal stating that tasks are implemented sequentially, one subagent per task, with parent review before the next task begins.
Record current SDK packed-artifact behaviour, SDK package manifest dependency protocols and API smoke commands before changing files.
Do not run bun run version-repo, remove private: true or publish unless the user explicitly asks for a release-prep/versioning slice.

globalVerification.commitPolicy: Commit after each coherent implementation slice passes mandatory verification, Changeset review or no-Changeset rationale, and parent audit.
requiredBeforeFinalPR: bun run changeset status --verbose when a Changeset exists, or explicit no-Changeset rationale for docs-only or app-internal slices
evidenceRequired: Current and final call graph summary for packed SDK validation, downstream workspace validation and live API smoke coverage.
Temp workspace path policy, cleanup policy and retained debug evidence policy.
Packed artifact and dependency install strategy, including unresolved workspace protocol findings.
Downstream typecheck evidence for valid examples and @ts-expect-error misuse.
Downstream runtime evidence for plain SDK and Effect SDK calculations.
Downstream browser-safe bundle evidence for root, AU and schemas entrypoints.
Live API smoke evidence for health, metadata, calculate and OpenAPI routes.
Changeset path and release-train impact, or explicit no-Changeset rationale.
Parent audit outcome for each task, including up to three correction turns when needed.

tasks.title: Audit current validation and design the downstream harness
tasks.goal: Confirm the current packed-artifact, package manifest and API smoke behaviour before adding a consumer workspace harness.
scope: Inspect packages/sdk/typescript/scripts/check-packed-artifact.ts, packages/sdk/typescript/package.json, packages/sdk/typescript/README.md and SDK type/runtime tests.
Inspect packages/api/http/package.json, packages/api/http README/tests and apps/api/scripts/smoke-public-routes.runtime.ts.
Record which package manifests still contain workspace:* dependency protocols and which packed artifacts are needed for an external install.
Decide whether the first implementation should install a packed dependency closure or fail with explicit workspace-protocol blocker diagnostics.
Create or update the active execution plan with current validation surfaces, chosen harness shape and verification gates.
Do not add runtime code in this task unless the audit proves a tiny script/package metadata change is required to make the next task reviewable.
outputs: Optional updates to docs/product-specs/downstream-consumer-validation.md if the audit discovers a different target call graph.
Optional updates to docs/product-specs/downstream-consumer-validation.tasks.json if implementation sequencing changes.

mandatorySubagentContract: You are implementing exactly one task from the WhatTax spec/task list.
Before editing, read the target spec, this task object, relevant current files, and the relevant docs in docs/architecture/*.
Identify the owning package for every schema, type, ID, service, layer and tagged error you touch.
Review the spec's call-graph diagrams before editing, and update or report them if implementation discovers a different final graph.
MUST use Effect-native primitives when they fit: Data, Schema, Array, Chunk, HashSet, HashMap, Match, Context, Layer, Config, Service, Record, Result, Exit, Bun, Platform, Command and ManagedRuntime.

Do not fall back to plain TypeScript control flow, nullable values, mutable records, mutable arrays, switch, Object.values, Object.entries, ad hoc _tag objects, unsafe casts, stringly branching, custom env parsing or hand-rolled runtime wrappers when an Effect/schema/platform primitive fits.
Reuse canonical schema-derived types, branded IDs, tagged errors, service contracts and constructors from the owning package.
Do not create mirrored DTOs, local duplicate types, local duplicate schemas or transport-only shape copies when an owning schema or API/SDK contract already exists.

Keep one-off Effect error handling and transformations inline at the callsite. Do not extract tiny mapper/wrapper helpers for single-use Effect.mapError, Effect.catchTag, Effect.catchAll or Effect.catchAllDefect.
Browser/runtime code must consume browser-safe API/SDK exports instead of importing server-only internals.
Package-facing changes MUST add or update a Changeset with bun run changeset, using a user-facing changelog summary. If the task is not package-facing, state that explicitly in the handoff.
Run this task's mandatory verification gates, including bun run verification unless the task explicitly documents a narrower gate.
Run task-specific tests, smoke checks, browser checks or architecture audits required by the task's blast radius.
For substantial code, API, SDK, app, package-boundary or docs-runtime work, run and document at least three implementation improvement audit passes before handoff.
Audit the diff for helper sprawl, canonical type/schema/id/error reuse, unsafe casts, local DTO mirrors, stringly branching and browser-safe imports where relevant.
Report the Changeset path and release-train impact, or report why no Changeset was required.
Report changed files, verification commands, outcomes and residual risks.
Report whether the final implementation still matches the spec's call graph.
Do not start or delegate another task. The parent agent must review, audit, verify and explicitly accept this task before the next task begins.
taskSpecificScope: Keep the audit grounded in current files and current package manifests.
Do not route runtime ownership to packages/scripts while it remains a placeholder.
Do not publish, version, remove private: true or run live package-name checks.
Do not name private downstream products in committed docs or evidence.

mandatoryVerification: Manifest audit documenting unresolved workspace:* dependencies and the chosen downstream install strategy.
Call-graph audit documenting whether the spec's target graphs still match the implementation plan.
bun run changeset or explicit no-Changeset rationale for docs/plan-only audit work
browserVerification: No browser-visible UI behaviour is expected to change.
Record the intended browser-safe downstream bundle entrypoints for later tasks.
qualityAudits: Audit pass 1: current validation call graphs are factual and package-owned.
Audit pass 2: planned harness ownership follows API/SDK, package-ownership and Effect-services docs.
Audit pass 3: planned verification gates cover packed install, typecheck, runtime SDK, browser-safe bundle and API smoke evidence without helper sprawl or private downstream names.
completionCriteria: The active execution plan records the current validation surfaces and downstream harness design.
Workspace protocol dependency findings are recorded as either release blockers or dependency-closure requirements.
The spec call graphs and task list still match the chosen implementation plan.
No package-facing code changed without a Changeset decision.
Parent agent reviewed the audit evidence, final call graph and no-Changeset or Changeset decision before accepting the task.

tasks.title: Add SDK external workspace validation
tasks.goal: Create the first deterministic downstream workspace proof for SDK package imports, typechecking, runtime calculations and browser-safe entrypoints.
scope: Add an SDK-owned downstream validation script under packages/sdk/typescript/scripts.
Add a package command, such as bun run --filter=@whattax/sdk validate:downstream.
Create a temp consumer workspace outside the repo and write package.json, tsconfig.json and example source files.
Use packed SDK artifacts and either a packed dependency closure or explicit release-blocker diagnostics for workspace:* dependency protocols.
Typecheck valid imports from @whattax/sdk, @whattax/sdk/effect, @whattax/sdk/au, @whattax/sdk/au/effect, @whattax/sdk/schemas and @whattax/sdk/testing.
Include at least one @ts-expect-error misuse that proves downstream compile-time rejection.
Run at least one plain SDK calculation and one Effect SDK calculation through public entrypoints.
Run a minimal browser-safe bundling or equivalent build check for root, AU and schemas entrypoints.
Print a compact evidence summary and clean up the temp workspace on success.

Report whether the final implementation still matches the spec's call graph.

taskSpecificScope: Keep the script SDK-owned and focused; do not create a generic packages/scripts runtime.
Use Effect/platform primitives for command execution, temp workspace lifecycle, cleanup and expected errors where they fit.
Do not mirror calculator DTOs in the downstream examples.
Do not silently ignore workspace:* dependency protocols in packed manifests.

mandatoryVerification: Downstream evidence audit documenting temp workspace location policy, install strategy, typecheck result, runtime SDK result, browser-safe bundle result and cleanup result.
rg audit proving browser-safe downstream examples do not import @whattax/api-http, app modules or server-only modules.
bun run changeset for package-facing script/README changes, or explicit no-Changeset rationale if the implementation is internal validation-only
browserVerification: Downstream browser-safe bundle or equivalent build check for @whattax/sdk, @whattax/sdk/au and @whattax/sdk/schemas.
No browser route check is required unless implementation touches apps/web or apps/docs UI routes.
qualityAudits: Audit pass 1: downstream SDK call graph uses public SDK entrypoints and no workspace source aliases.
Audit pass 2: Effect script flow is linear, uses platform primitives where they fit, keeps one-off error handling inline and avoids helper sprawl.
Audit pass 3: examples reuse canonical schemas/descriptors/errors, contain no local DTO mirrors or unsafe casts, and browser-safe imports do not leak server-only modules.
completionCriteria: The SDK package exposes a downstream validation command.
The command creates and validates a temp workspace outside the monorepo.
The command proves package import resolution, downstream typecheck, runtime SDK calculations and browser-safe bundling.
The command reports unresolved workspace:* protocols as release blockers or validates the packed dependency closure.
The temp workspace cleanup policy is deterministic and documented by command output.
Parent agent reviewed the diff, evidence output, Changeset decision and final call graph before accepting the task.

tasks.title: Add downstream HTTP and API validation
tasks.goal: Extend downstream proof to a real local apps/api process and public HTTP/API consumption without moving runtime ownership.
scope: Extend the downstream validation command or add a focused companion mode for HTTP/API validation.
Start apps/api with deterministic host/port config from the validation command or reuse the app-owned smoke command as the process owner.
Wait for /api/health, then call public metadata, calculate and OpenAPI routes from the external consumer workspace.
If @whattax/api-http client exports are validated downstream, use client/live or browser-safe client exports only; do not import server handlers or app modules.
Compare public response evidence against canonical SDK/API expectations without local DTO mirrors.
Stop the API process cleanly on success and failure.
Record route coverage, response checks, process cleanup and any unresolved release blockers in the active execution plan.
outputs: packages/sdk/typescript/package.json if command flags or scripts change
apps/api/README.md if the downstream/API smoke relationship is documented
packages/api/http/README.md if client downstream validation is documented

taskSpecificScope: Keep apps/api as the process lifecycle owner.
Do not leave API child processes running after validation failures.

mandatoryVerification: Live API cleanup audit proving downstream validation stops apps/api on success and failure.
HTTP route coverage audit documenting health, metadata, calculate and OpenAPI calls from the external workspace.
bun run changeset for package-facing command/client/README changes, or explicit no-Changeset rationale for app-internal/docs-only work
browserVerification: If @whattax/api-http browser-safe client exports are validated, run the downstream browser-safe bundle check against those exports.
No browser route check is required unless implementation touches apps/web or apps/docs UI routes.
qualityAudits: Audit pass 1: downstream HTTP call graph keeps apps/api runtime lifecycle, @whattax/api-http contract/client ownership and SDK dependency direction intact.
Audit pass 2: process lifecycle and cleanup use Effect/platform primitives where they fit and expected errors remain typed or explicitly bounded.
Audit pass 3: HTTP examples use public routes/client exports only, contain no local DTO mirrors or unsafe casts, and do not leak server-only imports into browser-safe code.
completionCriteria: Downstream validation proves public HTTP/API consumption against a real local apps/api process.
Health, metadata, calculate and OpenAPI routes are called from the external workspace.
The API process cleanup path works on success and failure.
The SDK still has no dependency on @whattax/api-http.
Parent agent reviewed the diff, API evidence, Changeset decision and final call graph before accepting the task.

tasks.title: Finalize release gate docs and evidence
tasks.goal: Make downstream validation part of the pre-publication release gate without publishing or versioning.
scope: Update package READMEs and public docs references that describe local release-readiness validation commands.
Update this spec and task list status if implementation is complete.
Update the active execution plan with final validation evidence and move it to completed if implementation is accepted.
Run the full validation story in the order a future release-prep operator should use.
Document any remaining blockers, especially workspace protocol dependency issues or live package-name checks that must wait for publication.
Do not run bun run version-repo, publish or remove private: true.
outputs: apps/api/README.md if release-gate command relationship changes
packages/api/http/README.md if HTTP client validation command relationship changes

taskSpecificScope: Keep public docs neutral and do not name private downstream products.
Do not broaden into generated docs inventory or package relocation work.
Do not publish, version or remove private: true.
Record package-name availability as a future live release-prep check, not as current static truth.

mandatoryVerification: bun run changeset status --verbose when a Changeset exists, or explicit no-Changeset rationale for docs-only/app-internal updates
Release gate audit proving private: true remains in place and bun run version-repo/npm publish were not run.
browserVerification: Downstream browser-safe bundle evidence remains current after docs and command updates.
No browser route check is required unless implementation touches apps/web or apps/docs UI routes.
qualityAudits: Audit pass 1: final spec, task list, READMEs and execution plan agree on the downstream validation call graph and release gate order.
Audit pass 2: final validation evidence covers packed install, typecheck, runtime SDK, browser-safe bundle, live API smoke, cleanup and blockers.
Audit pass 3: public docs are neutral, package boundaries remain intact, Changeset decisions are correct and no publish/version/private flag changes slipped in.
completionCriteria: Downstream validation is documented as a pre-publication release gate.
Final evidence is recorded in the completed execution plan.
Remaining blockers and future live package-name checks are explicit.
No public docs mention private downstream products.
private: true remains in package manifests and no publication/versioning action was taken.
Parent agent reviewed final docs, evidence, Changeset status and release gate integrity before accepting the task.
